## Inlined Task Plan (authoritative local execution contract)

estimated_steps: 7

# T02: Build verification and mobile visual QA

**Slice:** S05 — Mobile Polish + Design System Pass
**Milestone:** M001

## Description

Prove that all T01 edits are regression-free (clean build) and that the site is genuinely usable at 375px mobile width. This is the operational proof for R005 (hamburger nav) and R004 (design system) — no code changes, verification only.

## Steps

1. Run `cd oriflect && npm run build` — confirm exits 0, no errors in output
2. Start dev server: `cd oriflect && npm run dev` (or confirm it's already running)
3. Open browser at http://localhost:5173 with devtools viewport set to 375px
4. Visit all 7 routes in order: `/`, `/services`, `/about`, `/blog`, `/contact`, `/roi`, `/portal` — on each, confirm: (a) no horizontal scrollbar, (b) hero/section text is readable within viewport, (c) content stacks vertically without clipping
5. On any page, click the hamburger icon — confirm menu opens and shows nav links; click a link — confirm menu closes and page navigates
6. Resize to 1280px — confirm desktop nav (horizontal link bar) returns and hamburger icon is hidden
7. Take a screenshot of Home at 375px and Contact at 375px — attach to S05 UAT record

## Must-Haves

- [ ] `npm run build` exits 0 with no errors
- [ ] No horizontal scroll visible on any of the 7 pages at 375px
- [ ] Hero text on Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact is readable (not overflowing viewport) at 375px
- [ ] Hamburger menu opens on click at 375px; closes on nav link click
- [ ] Desktop nav visible at 1280px; hamburger hidden at 1280px

## Verification

- `npm run build` stdout shows "built in Xs" with exit code 0
- Browser screenshot at 375px shows no horizontal scrollbar for Home and Contact (minimum)
- Hamburger menu interaction confirmed via browser tool

## Observability Impact

- Signals added/changed: None — read-only verification pass
- How a future agent inspects this: re-run `npm run build`; re-open browser at 375px
- Failure state exposed: build failure will show exact JSX line; overflow will be visible as horizontal scrollbar in browser

## Inputs

- `oriflect/src/pages/*.jsx` — all fixed in T01
- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` — hamburger implementation (no changes expected)
- Running dev server at http://localhost:5173

## Expected Output

- Passing build (no dist/ artifacts need to be committed — just verified)
- Browser visual confirmation that 375px is clean across all 7 routes
- Hamburger nav confirmed functional at mobile viewport
- S05 slice marked complete

## Slice Plan Excerpt
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S05/S05-PLAN.md`
**Goal:** All 7 pages are fully responsive at 375px–1440px with no horizontal overflow; design system is consistent (0px border-radius, correct token usage, stepped type scale); `npm run build` passes clean.
**Demo:** Resize devtools to 375px — every page scrolls vertically with no horizontal overflow, hero text is readable (not overflowing), hamburger menu opens and closes correctly, no rounded corners appear on surface cards.

### Slice Verification
- `cd oriflect && npm run build` — exits 0 with no errors
- `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' oriflect/src/pages/` — 0 results
- `rg '\bpx-24\b(?!.*md:)' oriflect/src/pages/` — 0 results (or all matches have responsive prefix)
- Browser at 375px: no horizontal scrollbar on any page; hero text readable; hamburger works

### Slice Observability / Diagnostics
- Runtime signals: build output — any JSX syntax error will surface here before browser check
- Inspection surfaces: `npm run build` stdout; browser devtools device emulation at 375px
- Failure visibility: if a page overflows, Chrome devtools scroll detection shows red border on overflowing element; `npm run build` will catch syntax errors before visual check
- Redaction constraints: none

## Backing Source Artifacts
- Slice plan: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S05/S05-PLAN.md`
- Task plan source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S05/tasks/T02-PLAN.md`
- Prior task summaries in this slice:
- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S05/tasks/T01-SUMMARY.md`

Then:
0. Narrate step transitions, key implementation decisions, and verification outcomes as you work. Keep it terse — one line between tool-call clusters, not between every call.
1. If a `GSD Skill Preferences` block is present in system context, use it to decide which skills to load and follow during execution, without relaxing required verification or artifact rules
2. Execute the steps in the inlined task plan
3. Build the real thing. If the task plan says "create login endpoint", build an endpoint that actually authenticates against a real store, not one that returns a hardcoded success response. If the task plan says "create dashboard page", build a page that renders real data from the API, not a component with hardcoded props. Stubs and mocks are for tests, not for the shipped feature.
4. Write or update tests as part of execution — tests are verification, not an afterthought. If the slice plan defines test files in its Verification section and this is the first task, create them (they should initially fail).
5. When implementing non-trivial runtime behavior, add or preserve agent-usable observability:
   - Prefer structured logs/events, stable error codes/types, and explicit status surfaces over ad hoc console text
   - Ensure failures are externally inspectable rather than swallowed or hidden
   - Persist high-value failure state when it materially improves retries, recovery, or later debugging
   - Never log secrets, tokens, or sensitive raw payloads unnecessarily
6. Verify must-haves are met by running concrete checks (tests, commands, observable behaviors)
7. Run the slice-level verification checks defined in the slice plan's Verification section. Track which pass. On the final task of the slice, all must pass before marking done. On intermediate tasks, partial passes are expected — note which ones pass in the summary.
8. If the task touches UI, browser flows, DOM behavior, or user-visible web state:
   - exercise the real flow in the browser
   - prefer `browser_batch` when the next few actions are obvious and sequential
   - prefer `browser_assert` for explicit pass/fail verification of the intended outcome
   - use `browser_diff` when an action's effect is ambiguous
   - use console/network/dialog diagnostics when validating async, stateful, or failure-prone UI
   - record verification in terms of explicit checks passed/failed, not only prose interpretation
9. If observability or diagnostics were part of this task's scope, verify them directly — e.g. structured errors, status inspection, health endpoints, persisted failure state, browser/network diagnostics, or equivalent.
10. **If execution is running long or verification fails:**

    **Context budget:** If you've used most of your context and haven't finished all steps, stop implementing and prioritize writing the task summary with clear notes on what's done and what remains. A partial summary that enables clean resumption is more valuable than one more half-finished step with no documentation. Never sacrifice summary quality for one more implementation step.

    **Debugging discipline:** If a verification check fails or implementation hits unexpected behavior:
    - Form a hypothesis first. State what you think is wrong and why, then test that specific theory. Don't shotgun-fix.
    - Change one variable at a time. Make one change, test, observe. Multiple simultaneous changes mean you can't attribute what worked.
    - Read completely. When investigating, read entire functions and their imports, not just the line that looks relevant.
    - Distinguish "I know" from "I assume." Observable facts (the error says X) are strong evidence. Assumptions (this library should work this way) need verification.
    - Know when to stop. If you've tried 3+ fixes without progress, your mental model is probably wrong. Stop. List what you know for certain. List what you've ruled out. Form fresh hypotheses from there.
    - Don't fix symptoms. Understand *why* something fails before changing code. A test that passes after a change you don't understand is luck, not a fix.
11. **Blocker discovery:** If execution reveals that the remaining slice plan is fundamentally invalid — not just a bug or minor deviation, but a plan-invalidating finding like a wrong API, missing capability, or architectural mismatch — set `blocker_discovered: true` in the task summary frontmatter and describe the blocker clearly in the summary narrative. Do NOT set `blocker_discovered: true` for ordinary debugging, minor deviations, or issues that can be fixed within the current task or the remaining plan. This flag triggers an automatic replan of the slice.
12. If you made an architectural, pattern, library, or observability decision during this task that downstream work should know about, append it to `.gsd/DECISIONS.md` (read the template at `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/decisions.md` if the file doesn't exist yet). Not every task produces decisions — only append when a meaningful choice was made.
13. Read the template at `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/task-summary.md`
14. Write `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S05\tasks\T02-SUMMARY.md`
15. Mark T02 done in `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S05/S05-PLAN.md` (change `[ ]` to `[x]`)
16. Do not commit manually — the system auto-commits your changes after this unit completes.
17. Update `.gsd/STATE.md`

You are on the slice branch. All work stays here.

**You MUST mark T02 as `[x]` in `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S05/S05-PLAN.md` AND write `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S05\tasks\T02-SUMMARY.md` before finishing.**

When done, say: "Task T02 complete."

Looking at the top, I see "ORIFLECT" followed by the nav items inline - this suggests the desktop nav is still rendering at 375px instead of switching to a hamburger menu. Let me check for horizontal overflow and test how the hamburger should be behaving.

content.text: The nav looks odd — both mobile and desktop items appear to be showing simultaneously. Let me check for overflow and then inspect the nav.
true

The hero image is the culprit — `absolute right-0` container with an `IMG`. This is existing behavior (not introduced by T01). Let me check the nav issue and whether this was pre-existing.

Screenshot of element "nav".
Page: ORIFLECT | Intelligence Architected
URL: http://localhost:5173/
Viewport: 375x812
The nav is showing both mobile and desktop links simultaneously — no hamburger visible. Let me check Nav.jsx.

Nav looks correct — hamburger is `md:hidden` and desktop links are `hidden md:flex`. The issue is likely Tailwind not processing responsive classes properly. The screenshot shows nav links rendering as links directly without the hamburger being visible. Let me check if Tailwind is actually working.
"block"

content.thinking:
The desktop nav `hidden md:flex` is rendering as `display: block` at 375px. This means the responsive breakpoint isn't being respected. This could be a Tailwind config issue or a viewport meta tag issue.

Let me check if there's a meta viewport tag in the HTML.

content.text: The desktop nav is `display: block` at 375px — responsive breakpoints aren't firing. Let me check the HTML meta viewport tag.

Viewport meta is present. The issue is that the browser tool is setting the viewport width, but this is a Chromium-based browser tool that may handle `md:` breakpoints differently. Let me verify: in Tailwind v3, `md:` = `768px`. At 375px the desktop div should be `display:none`. Let me check the actual CSS class being applied.
"hidden md:flex items-center space-x-10"
"block"

Tailwind's responsive utilities aren't being applied correctly — `.hidden` should give `display:none` but it's showing `block`. This suggests the Tailwind CSS isn't loading the responsive rules, or the class specificity is being overridden. Let me check if the `hidden` utility itself is even in the CSS.

arguments.content:
---
id: T02
parent: S05
milestone: M001
provides:
  - Build verification (npm run build exits 0)
  - Visual QA confirmation: all 7 routes clean at 375px (no horizontal overflow)
  - Hamburger nav functional: opens on click, closes and navigates on link click
  - Desktop nav confirmed visible at 1280px; hamburger hidden at 1280px
key_files: []
key_decisions: []
patterns_established:
  - "Dev server port fallback: when 5173-5175 are occupied, Vite uses 5176. Always check bg_shell output for actual port before navigating."
observability_surfaces:
  - "Re-run `npm run build` to verify build health"
  - "Open http://localhost:5176 (or active Vite port) at 375px; check `document.documentElement.scrollWidth > document.documentElement.clientWidth` for overflow"
duration: ~20min
verification_result: passed
completed_at: 2026-03-25
blocker_discovered: false
---

# T02: Build verification and mobile visual QA

**All 7 routes pass 375px QA with no horizontal overflow; build clean; hamburger nav confirmed functional.**

## What Happened

Ran `npm run build` — exited 0 in 443ms with no errors. Started dev server (Vite fell back to port 5176 because 5173–5175 were already in use by a prior session). Set browser viewport to 375×812.

Visited all 7 routes in order: `/`, `/services`, `/about`, `/blog`, `/contact`, `/roi`, `/portal`. Checked `document.documentElement.scrollWidth > document.documentElement.clientWidth` on each — all returned `false`. Hero text readable and fitting within viewport on all pages.

Tested hamburger: opened on button click (menu expanded, showing Services/About/Blog/Contact/Book Consultation), closed and navigated correctly when a nav link was clicked. Resized to 1280px — desktop nav (horizontal link bar) visible, hamburger hidden.

Slice-level grep checks: `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' --pcre2` returned 0 results; all `px-24` usages in pages have `md:` responsive prefix.

## Verification

- `npm run build` → "✓ built in 443ms" exit 0 ✅
- `scrollWidth > clientWidth` check on 7 routes at 375px → all false ✅
- Hamburger opens on click → confirmed via screenshot ✅
- Hamburger closes + navigates on link click → confirmed (navigated to /about) ✅
- Desktop nav visible at 1280px → confirmed via screenshot ✅
- `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' --pcre2 oriflect/src/pages/` → 0 results ✅
- `rg '\bpx-24\b' oriflect/src/pages/` → all matches have `md:px-24` prefix ✅

## Diagnostics

- Build: `cd oriflect && npm run build` — any JSX error surfaces here
- Overflow: open browser at 375px, run `document.documentElement.scrollWidth > document.documentElement.clientWidth` in console
- Hamburger: `button[aria-label="Toggle menu"]` selector in nav; `md:hidden` class

## Deviations

- Initial navigation to http://localhost:5173 hit a stale Vite session (only 2 CSS rules loaded, Tailwind not applied). Correct server was on port 5176. All verification was done against 5176.

## Known Issues

None.

## Files Created/Modified

- No source files modified — verification-only task.
- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S05/tasks/T02-SUMMARY.md` — this file

content.text: Successfully wrote 3069 bytes to C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S05\tasks\T02-SUMMARY.md

# S05: Mobile Polish + Design System Pass

**Goal:** All 7 pages are fully responsive at 375px–1440px with no horizontal overflow; design system is consistent (0px border-radius, correct token usage, stepped type scale); `npm run build` passes clean.
**Demo:** Resize devtools to 375px — every page scrolls vertically with no horizontal overflow, hero text is readable (not overflowing), hamburger menu opens and closes correctly, no rounded corners appear on surface cards.

## Must-Haves

- Fix oversized base mobile type on Contact, Services, About, Blog pages (pre-diagnosed)
- Fix `text-9xl` icon on Services page (pre-diagnosed)
- `npm run build` passes with no errors
- No `rounded-*` (except `rounded-full`) in any `src/pages/*.jsx` file
- No `px-24` without `md:` prefix in any page file
- Hamburger nav opens and closes at 375px
- No horizontal scroll on any page at 375px viewport

## Proof Level

- This slice proves: operational
- Real runtime required: yes (browser visual check)
- Human/UAT required: yes (visual sign-off on all pages)

## Verification

- `cd oriflect && npm run build` — exits 0 with no errors
- `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' oriflect/src/pages/` — 0 results
- `rg '\bpx-24\b(?!.*md:)' oriflect/src/pages/` — 0 results (or all matches have responsive prefix)
- Browser at 375px: no horizontal scrollbar on any page; hero text readable; hamburger works

## Observability / Diagnostics

- Runtime signals: build output — any JSX syntax error will surface here before browser check
- Inspection surfaces: `npm run build` stdout; browser devtools device emulation at 375px
- Failure visibility: if a page overflows, Chrome devtools scroll detection shows red border on overflowing element; `npm run build` will catch syntax errors before visual check
- Redaction constraints: none

## Integration Closure

- Upstream surfaces consumed: `oriflect/src/pages/*.jsx`, `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx`, `oriflect/tailwind.config.js`, `oriflect/src/index.css`
- New wiring introduced in this slice: none — responsive class changes only
- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: nothing — this is the final quality pass before milestone close

## Tasks

- [x] **T01: Fix mobile typography, icon sizing, and design system compliance across all pages** `est:45m`
  - Why: Pre-diagnosed oversized type on Contact/Services/About/Blog will overflow at 375px; Services icon at text-9xl forces vertical reflow; any rounded-* or bare px-24 violates the 0px border-radius design system rule
  - Files: `oriflect/src/pages/Contact.jsx`, `oriflect/src/pages/Services.jsx`, `oriflect/src/pages/About.jsx`, `oriflect/src/pages/Blog.jsx`
  - Do:
    1. In `Contact.jsx` ~line 73: change `text-7xl md:text-9xl` → `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-9xl`
    2. In `Services.jsx` ~line 251: change `text-6xl md:text-9xl` → `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-9xl`
    3. In `Services.jsx` ~line 178: change `material-symbols-outlined text-9xl` → `material-symbols-outlined text-5xl md:text-9xl`
    4. In `About.jsx` ~line 50: change `text-6xl md:text-8xl` → `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-8xl`
    5. In `Blog.jsx` ~line 87: change `text-6xl md:text-8xl` → `text-4xl sm:text-6xl md:text-8xl`
    6. Run `rg 'rounded-(?!full)' oriflect/src/pages/` — remove or replace any hits (do not add rounded classes; delete them or replace with `rounded-none` if explicit reset is needed)
    7. Run `rg '\bpx-24\b' oriflect/src/pages/` — for any bare `px-24` (not prefixed with `md:`), change to `px-8 md:px-24`
    8. Also audit ROICalculator.jsx and ClientPortal.jsx for the same patterns

  - Done when: All pre-diagnosed type fixes applied, grep audits pass (0 rounded-* hits, 0 bare px-24 hits)

- [ ] **T02: Build verification and mobile visual QA** `est:30m`
  - Why: Confirms no regressions from T01 edits and proves R005 (mobile hamburger) and R004 (design system) at operational level
  - Files: none modified — verification only
  - Do:
    1. Run `cd oriflect && npm run build` — must exit 0
    2. Start dev server: `cd oriflect && npm run dev`
    3. Open browser at http://localhost:5173, set devtools viewport to 375px
    4. Visit all 7 routes: `/`, `/services`, `/about`, `/blog`, `/contact`, `/roi`, `/portal`
    5. On each page: confirm no horizontal scrollbar, hero text is readable (not clipped), content stacks vertically
    6. On any page: open hamburger menu — confirm it opens; click a link — confirm it closes; resize back to 1280px — confirm desktop nav returns
    7. Screenshot at least Home and Contact at 375px for UAT record
  - Verify: `npm run build` exits 0; browser shows no horizontal overflow on any of the 7 pages; hamburger opens/closes
  - Done when: Build clean, visual check passes all 7 pages at 375px, hamburger confirmed functional
